spec_match.rationale: Page is a code-snippet card with no live invocation of the API the spec describes, so the spec behaviour is asserted but never demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: No interactive controls of substance — the page reads as a static code card, which CLAUDE.md rule 7 explicitly forbids.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Page surfaces multiple failure / unsupported / edge-case branches in the UI, not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/6090916291674112 self-heal link (CLAUDE.md rule 3).

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Add a real interactive control surface
openQuestions.detail: The concept page currently reads more like a write-up than a hands-on demo. Add a control that drives the underlying API (or a faithful simulation) and surfaces the live result on the page — buttons, sliders, selects, or a try-it form. CLAUDE.md rule 7 explicitly bans static code cards as concept pages.

openQuestions.title: Exercise more of the On-device Web Speech API spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The spec (https://webaudio.github.io/web-speech-api) covers more than the page demonstrates. Walk back through the spec's exposed methods, properties, or modes and add controls for the ones that materially change behaviour. Today the page only hints at a subset.

openQuestions.title: Mirror the spec's stated motivation in the demo
openQuestions.detail: ChromeStatus motivation reads: 'The Web Speech API is a web standard API that allows developers to incorporate speech recognition and synthesis into their web pages. Currently, the Web Speech API spec does not mention where speech recognition occurs, and most implementers...'. The demo should make that motivation visible — show the problem the spec is solving (before/after, with-without, attacker view) so a reader leaves understanding why the feature ships, not just what it does.

summary: 'Availability & Language Matrix' misses a non-trivial part of the rubric (2 fail / 0 partial). The open questions list captures the highest-leverage fixes — usually adding a real interactive control or wiring the chromestatus reference link.
